content: By integrating multiple inconsistent solving perspectives from LLM self-reflection mechanisms into the unguided iterative self-feedback loop of Self-Refine, we can build a multi-perspective self-refinement framework that eliminates the need for external supervision or training while significantly reducing the risk of the LLM reinforcing its own inherent biases during iteration. This framework leverages conflicting self-generated perspectives to surface blind spots the original single-perspective Self-Refine would miss, without adding any additional training overhead.
